hi-vis-yellow.$description: Hi-Vis Yellow — Primary action buttons, wordmark, navigation pill borders, section badges, equalizer bars, brand asterisk, highlight underlines — the only chromatic color in the system, carrying 100% of brand voice and functional energy

ink-black.$description: Ink Black — Primary page background — a warm near-black that sits behind every screen, softer than pure black and warmer against the yellow

pure-black.$description: Pure Black — Hard borders, image outlines, logo grid dividers, text on yellow fills — the structural ink that gives the system its edges

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text color, body copy, headings, client logos, light surface fills — a warm off-white that reads as paper against the warm-black ground

charcoal.$description: Charcoal — Subtle dividers, secondary borders, low-contrast structural lines between grid cells

sand.$description: Sand — Muted helper text, secondary descriptive copy, low-emphasis labels

apercu-pro.$description: Display and body sans-serif — carries the CASSETTE wordmark at 56–60px, section headings at 37px, and body text at 15px. The single weight (400) used across all sizes is deliberate: hierarchy comes from size jumps, not weight shifts, giving the type a calm, even voice.

apercu-mono-pro.$description: Monospace workhorse for all UI chrome: navigation pills, section badges, city labels, button text, footer, and small body annotations. The monospace proportions give every interactive element a technical, label-like quality — the chrome feels like equipment markings rather than marketing type.

ink-black-canvas.$description: Surface level 1: Primary page background across all sections

bone-surface.$description: Surface level 2: Inverse surface — text and logo fills sitting on the dark canvas

hi-vis-accent.$description: Surface level 3: Functional accent surface for pills, badges, and active marks
